column5: Behind The Cape - Snowflake Apache Iceberg  and Polaris - Saurabh Kumar
column9: Behind the Cape - Apache Iceberg and Polaris Final.mp4
column16: Behind The Cape - Snowflake Apache Iceberg  and Polaris
column17:
In this episode of Behind the Cape, Data Superhero Keith Belanger hosts fellow Data Superhero Saurabh Kumar as he breaks down how Snowflake, Apache Iceberg, and Apache Polaris are coming together to redefine what a modern, interoperable lakehouse really looks like.

They chat through why open table formats and open catalogs matter more than ever, how Snowflake integrates with Iceberg and Polaris to support cross-cloud interoperability, and how teams can maintain vendor neutrality without sacrificing enterprise-grade performance, security, or governance.

column16: Snowflake Postgres In 2 Minutes: Fully Managed Postgres On Snowflake
column17:
Snowflake Postgres brings the world’s most popular open source database onto the Snowflake platform in a fully managed way. See how it unifies transactional and analytical workloads, and how developers can get started in minutes directly from Snowsight.
The demo walks through provisioning an instance, connecting with standard Postgres tools, and using familiar features like extensions and permissions. You also learn how Snowflake Postgres simplifies operations with built in insights for performance, storage, and long running queries. Find out more: https://www.snowflake.com/postgres

column5: DuckDB querying Snowflake-managed Apache Iceberg via Horizon (Iceberg REST) Catalog
column16: Query Snowflake Managed Iceberg Tables With DuckDB
column17:
This demo shows how to query Snowflake managed Apache Iceberg tables using DuckDB through the Snowflake Horizon Iceberg REST Catalog. 

What you'll learn:
• Set up Snowflake's Horizon Catalog for external engine access
• Configure DuckDB with PAT (Programmatic Access Token) authentication
• Query Iceberg tables from DuckDB with zero data movement
• Handle RBAC and common troubleshooting scenarios

Resources: https://github.com/kameshsampath/hirc-duckdb-demo

column5: Snowflake Intelligence + Domo: Marketing Mix Modeling Integration
column9: 26-PRG - Snowflake Intelligence + Domo - Marketing Mix Modeling Integration_012726.mp4
column16: Conversational Marketing Mix Modeling With Snowflake Intelligence
column17:
Andreina Toledo, Partner Solutions Consultant at Domo, demonstrates how to build conversational AI for Marketing Mix Modeling using Domo and Snowflake Intelligence.

Marketing teams are constantly asked which channels truly drive results. Traditional platform metrics show clicks and conversions but cannot separate correlation from causation or measure true incremental impact. Domo MMM addresses this gap using Bayesian statistical modeling to analyze historical marketing spend alongside revenue, accounting for seasonality, carryover effects, and diminishing returns to deliver confidence-based ROI insights.

What sets Domo MMM apart is how teams interact with these insights. By integrating with Snowflake Intelligence, Domo adds a conversational layer on top of MMM results. Marketers can ask natural language questions like which channels to prioritize or why spend should shift and receive clear, actionable answers grounded directly in the underlying analysis.


Learn More: https://www.snowflake.com/en/developers/guides/domo-mmm-guide/

column5: Keyless Data Engineering: AWS Lambda & Snowflake WIDF Explained
column16: Keyless Data Engineering With Snowflake And AWS Lambda
column17:
Learn how to build a secure, serverless data pipeline using Snowpark Python, AWS Lambda, and Snowflake Workload Identity Federation. 

You will see how an S3 event triggers Lambda, how temporary credentials are exchanged using identity federation, and how data is written securely into Snowflake. The walkthrough covers deployment, trust configuration, and validation, ending with Snowflake Dynamic Tables automatically refreshing as new data arrives. This pattern combines strong security with serverless speed for modern data engineering workflows.

👉 Get the demo code and documentation:
https://github.com/kameshsampath/snowpark-widf-lambda-loader-demo

column17:
Bart Hofkin, Systems Architect at Akave, walks through how to integrate Akave Cloud with Snowflake.

In this demo, you’ll see how to create an Akave bucket, configure it as an external stage in Snowflake, and read and write data between Snowflake and Akave using standard Snowflake workflows.

This video is a step-by-step walkthrough focused on setup and usage, showing how S3-compatible object storage can be used directly within Snowflake.

For more details and written instructions, refer to the Akave + Snowflake integration guide:
https://akave.com/blog/akave-cloud-the-first-decentralized-object-storage-integrated-with-snowflake

column5: BUILD London Cortex Code Breakout Session
column15: Cortex Code: Agents for Snowflake Development
column16: Cortex Code: The Purpose Built Agent for Snowflake Development
column17:
The process of building production-ready applications can have a lot of friction points - wrestling with connections, debugging permissions, and generating repetitive, complex configurations. If you spend more time managing the platform than writing business logic, your productivity is suffering. We believe your terminal should be a launchpad for innovation, not a place to solve administrative puzzles.

This is the core mission of Cortex Code. This session introduces the new agentic CLI that puts the collective expertise of Snowflake directly into your command line. Through a rapid-fire live demonstration, we’ll show you how to use simple, natural language commands to instantly scaffold entire Snowpark projects, generate secure SQL, and orchestrate complex multi-step deployments. Discover how Cortex Code becomes your resident expert, allowing you to focus purely on value creation.

See how Cortex Code CLI helps you ship workflows faster, integrate data systems, and build with confidence using natural language.

column16: Optimize dbt Projects on Snowflake for Speed With Cortex Code
column17:
dbt projects on Snowflake are easy to build, but keeping them fast and cost-efficient is another challenge. As source tables grow and models become more complex, runtimes increase and performance issues get harder to pinpoint. 

In this demo, you'll see how to direct Cortex Code CLI to analyze your project’s run_results.json, identify the slowest models, flag unused ones, and find suggestions for specific optimizations you can apply immediately, all from a single prompt in your terminal.

column16: Data Product Enrichment With Zero-ETL Transformations: Resharing GA
column17:
Resharing, a new Snowflake AI & Data sharing feature that builds on our Zero-ETL, cross-cloud and cross-region capabilities, allows customers to reuse shared data products without having to manage local copies (materialize) and apply light-weight transformations on the fly. 

Resharing can help customers simplify their sharing architecture by providing simple, light-weight transformation for shared data product enrichment, without having to move, extract or load the data.

column16: Make Your dbt Core Builds Faster with Cortex Code
column17:
When dbt builds fail or pipelines slow down, Cortex Code CLI accelerates troubleshooting by bringing Snowflake expertise directly to your terminal. By reading your project files—including models, tests, and lineage—without requiring a manual compile, it can instantly identify bottlenecks, perform blast radius analysis, and audit test coverage. 

This data-native AI agent combines dbt project structure with Snowflake query history to provide a prioritized improvement plan, allowing you to trace dependencies and refactor code from a single prompt with total confidence.

column16: Ship AI Agents Fast with Cortex Code CLI

In this walkthrough, we use Cortex Code CLI to build a Cortex Agent powered directly by governed Snowflake data. We combine structured metrics, unstructured search, routing logic, and guardrails, then deploy to Snowflake Intelligence.

column5: Bi-directional Interoperability with Horizon Catalog demo
column16: Bi-Directional Interoperability With Snowflake Horizon Catalog
column17:
Learn how Snowflake Horizon Catalog enables bi-directional interoperability across your open lakehouse architecture.

In this demo, Riccardo Muti shows how to create and manage Apache Iceberg tables in Snowflake, access them from external engines like Spark, and securely read and write data using the Iceberg REST Catalog API. You also see the reverse scenario: federating external catalogs such as AWS Glue into Snowflake for seamless query and write access.

column5: Data and AI Governance with Horizon Catalog
column16: Horizon Catalog: Governing Data For Trusted AI
column17:
Learn how Horizon Catalog detects and protects sensitive data, enforces tag-based masking and automatic tag propagation, and delivers observability for lineage and data quality so AI can run safely on trusted data.

This demo shows practical governance workflows in Snowflake. Creating classification profiles, applying tag-based masking policies, tracing column-level lineage, and using universal search and conversational discovery to find and understand data.

column16: Semantic Views In Horizon Catalog
column17:
Learn how to create and use semantic views in Snowflake Horizon Catalog to make data understandable and actionable for both humans and AI. 

This video demonstrates how Cortex Analyst turns business prompts into correct SQL using semantic views, how to enrich metadata with descriptions, relationships, verified queries, and custom metrics, and how to expose agents via MCP so external AI tools can query your semantic models securely.

column16: What is Snowflake? Developer Demo (2026)
column17:
Snowflake is a unified, fully-managed, multi-cloud data and AI platform that lets you build data pipelines, train ML models, and deploy AI-powered applications, and more, all without managing infrastructure. In this complete walkthrough, we'll show you how developers, data engineers, and ML teams use Snowflake to go from raw data to production-ready insights.

Whether you're building your first data pipeline or scaling enterprise ML workloads, this video covers everything you need to get started.

🔗 Start your free trial: https://signup.snowflake.com/developers

0:00 - Introduction
